summary: Full E2E test of Mental Health AI MVP (FastAPI + React + Firebase Auth + SQLite). All 22 backend pytest tests pass. Frontend flows for patient (signup, mood, journal with AI emotion, chat, analytics, book, logout), doctor (signup, registration, pending), and admin (signup, console, approve doctor) all work end-to-end. Cross-flow verified: approved doctor surfaces in public /api/doctor/list.

integration_issues.issue: New users are never redirected to /role — the backend's User model defaults role='patient', so HomeRedirect's `!profile?.role` check is always false. Users who want to be a doctor/admin must know to manually navigate to /role. RoleSelection.jsx works when accessed, and the /api/user/set-role endpoint accepts the change correctly, but the happy-path UI for non-patient signups is broken.

The nav link labelled 'Assessments' goes to /patient/assessment (singular). Direct navigation to /patient/assessments (plural) falls through to PatientDashboard because no catch-all /patient/* route exists. Low impact but inconsistent (nav label is plural, URL is singular).

action_items: Consider redirecting brand-new signups to /role before defaulting role='patient' (either remove default in DB and use null, or let /api/user/me return role=null on first create so the frontend redirects to /role and the user explicitly picks patient/doctor/admin).
Align patient assessment route/nav: rename route to /patient/assessments OR change nav link label to singular 'Assessment' to match URL.
critical_code_review_comments: User.role column defaults to 'patient' in /app/backend/app/models/user.py — this silently bypasses the role selection UX for doctor/admin signups.
No /patient/* fallback route — misspelled patient URLs silently render PatientDashboard instead of 404.
/api/user/set-role accepts any role incl. 'admin' without restriction (noted as intentional-for-demo in test_credentials.md, but flag for production).
CORS is `allow_origins=['*']` with `allow_credentials=True` in server.py — browsers ignore this combo; fine for demo but invalid for prod.
test_credentials: All created at test time. Latest: patient e2e_patient_1776794537@aura-test.dev / Patient123!, doctor e2e_doctor_1776794648@aura-test.dev / Password123! (approved), admin e2e_admin_1776794648@aura-test.dev / Password123!
seed_data_creation: One approved doctor now exists in SQLite (id=1, name='test-field') — leave in place so future tests can book appointments against it.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Approved doctor id=1 is seeded in SQLite via admin approval flow. Patient booking an appointment against this doctor was NOT exercised (only empty-state was tested before approval). Recommend retest to confirm slots endpoint and POST /api/appointments/ works for a real patient+doctor pair. Firebase auth works headlessly via email/password; Google button only rendered (do not click). All pytest assertions are green and committed in /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py.
rca of the issue: N/A — no blocking bugs found. Two UX/design concerns reported as action items.
